pattern: Prefix sums of candy counts bound how many candies must precede a type, so each query reduces to an interval overlap between the eating range and the type's candy range.
hints: Before reaching type t you must finish all candies of types 0..t-1, giving a minimum and maximum count eaten by a given day.
By day d you have eaten between d+1 (one per day) and (d+1)*cap candies; the type spans [prefix[t]+1, prefix[t+1]] in eating order.
Answer is true iff those two intervals overlap: prefix[t] < (day+1)*cap and day+1 <= prefix[t+1].

edge_inputs.description: second sample mixing true and false
single type single candy on day zero
favorite day too early to reach the type
huge cap lets you reach far types immediately
cap one forces exactly one per day
last type on its earliest possible day

stress.description: many random candy counts and queries within bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    counts = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    q = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    queries = []
    for _ in range(q):
        t = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        day = rng.randint(0, 5000)
        cap = rng.randint(1, 1000)
        queries.append([t, day, cap])
    return [counts, queries]
